steps.title: Translate the constraint into an equation
steps.content:
Carmen needs at least 100 signatures total. She has 51. We need to find the minimum number of additional signatures she requires.

If she has 51 and needs at least 100, then:
$$51 + x \geq 100$$
where $x$ is the number of additional signatures.

steps.title: Solve for the minimum additional signatures
steps.content:
For the minimum, we set the inequality to equality (she needs exactly 100 at minimum):
$$51 + x = 100$$
$$x = 100 - 51 = 49$$
Therefore, Carmen needs exactly 49 additional signatures to reach the 100-signature minimum. That matches choice C — C is correct.
